headline: The Bull Case For DoorDash (DASH) Could Change Following ALSO Autonomous EV Partnership - Learn Why
summary: In March 2026, ALSO, a Palo Alto-based small electric vehicle developer spun out of Rivian, announced a multi-year partnership and investment agreement with DoorDash to co-develop and scale autonomous last-mile delivery using its purpose-built EVs, with DoorDash gaining board representation through DoorDash Labs co-founder Stanley Tang as a board observer. This move deepens DoorDash’s push into automation and purpose-built delivery vehicles, potentially reshaping how it handles last-mile...

headline: Manna Air Delivery Raises $50Million Series B as It Announces Plans to Expand in the United States
summary: DUBLIN, April 01, 2026--Manna Air Delivery, a global leader in consumer drone delivery, has announced a $50 million funding round to scale its proven operations further in the United States and Europe. The round brings Manna’s total funding to $110million. Manna now operates one of the most active consumer drone delivery networks in the world, with more than 250,000 regulated commercial UAV flights completed.

summary: Micromobility startup Also secures new funding and teams with DoorDash to develop autonomous last-mile delivery vehicles.

headline: ‘I’m done’: Rideshare drivers on the brink of quitting over higher gas prices
summary: Millions of Uber, Lyft, DoorDash and Instacart drivers have no choice but to pay higher gas prices if they want to keep earning a living.

headline: Rivian’s Also DoorDash Deal Tests Autonomy Platform Beyond Vehicle Sales
summary: Rivian spinoff Also secured a $200 million Series C funding round linked to a new partnership with DoorDash. The multi year agreement will deploy small autonomous EVs for deliveries across selected markets. DoorDash will take a board role at Also, aligning the delivery platform with Rivian related autonomy and EV technology. headline: Rivian’s E-Bike Spinoff Scores Billion-Dollar Valuation
summary: The e-bike industry has spent the last year in a Chapter 11 shredder, but a new “War Economy” at the pump is changing the math.
